name: RISC-V: sum of an integer array
task: Create /tmp/eval_rvsum/code.s containing RV32I assembly for a function `main` that computes the sum of a signed-int32 array. Calling convention: a0 = base address, a1 = length (count of int32 elements). Return the sum in a0. The validator runs your code via the bundled RV32I interpreter (/tmp/eval_rvsum/rvi.py) with a0/a1/memory pre-initialized.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('rvi','/tmp/eval_rvsum/rvi.py'); rvi = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(rvi); src = open('/tmp/eval_rvsum/code.s').read(); arr = [10, -20, 30, 40, -5, 100]; base = 200; mem = [(base + 4*i, v & 0xFFFFFFFF) for i,v in enumerate(arr)]; cpu = rvi.run(src, regs={'a0': base, 'a1': len(arr)}, mem_init=mem); got = cpu.signed(rvi.REGS['a0']); assert got == sum(arr), f'got {got} expected {sum(arr)}'; cpu2 = rvi.run(src, regs={'a0': 0, 'a1': 0}); assert cpu2.signed(rvi.REGS['a0']) == 0; print('OK')
